text:
Once upon a time in a small seaside town, there was a peculiar postman named Harold. Harold wasn't your ordinary mail carrier. He harbored a secret talent; Harold could read people's minds almost effortlessly.

One crisp autumn afternoon, Harold was sorting letters on the porch when he noticed a timid old woman peeking through the window. Her eyes were filled with a sadness that no words could express.

Harold glanced at the mailbox. No letters there.

"Hello," he said calmly, approaching her.

She turned, her eyes dropping as the weight of past memories came crashing down.

"Lost your grandson?" Harold asked softly.

She nodded, a tear dribbling down her cheek.

"Why didn't you send him photos?" Harold suggested.

Her shoulders shakes. She told him of the accident.

Harald listened intently. Moments later, a letter slipped out of his pocket.

"Pictures," he said kindly. "Let's take some."
